_comment: Common GPUs and their architecture identifiers: NVIDIA compute capability (sm), AMD gfx target, or Intel/Apple family. Used by the GPU lookup on the site. Optional per-GPU constraints: only_os / only_arch restrict matching builds (e.g. Jetson is Linux aarch64 only); note is shown when the GPU is selected.

gpus.name: NVIDIA Jetson Xavier
gpus.note: Jetson devices need NVIDIA's JetPack-specific PyTorch wheels — the aarch64 wheels on download.pytorch.org are built for SBSA servers (e.g. GH200), not Jetson.

gpus.name: NVIDIA T4 / RTX 20 series / GTX 16 series
NVIDIA Jetson Orin
gpus.note: Jetson devices need NVIDIA's JetPack-specific PyTorch wheels — the aarch64 wheels on download.pytorch.org are built for SBSA servers (e.g. GH200), not Jetson.

gpus.name: NVIDIA RTX 40 series / L4 / L40 / L40S
NVIDIA RTX 50 series / RTX PRO Blackwell
AMD Instinct MI50 / MI60 / Radeon VII
AMD RX 6800/6900 series / PRO W6800 / PRO V620
AMD RX 7900 XTX/XT/GRE / PRO W7900 / PRO W7800
AMD RX 7800 XT / RX 7700 XT / PRO W7700 / PRO V710
AMD RX 9070 series / AI PRO R9700
Intel Core Ultra integrated Arc GPU
Intel Data Center GPU Max (Ponte Vecchio)
Apple Silicon (M series)
gpus.note: macOS arm64 CPU wheels include MPS (Metal) GPU acceleration for PyTorch ≥ 1.12 — no extra install needed.
